digest.top_line: mRCAT-III: pCR 61% vs 29% (p<0.0001); tislelizumab + node-sparing SCRT doubles pCR in pMMR LARC.
digest.tldr: Lower-GI led the day: mRCAT-III delivers a striking IO-augmented pCR signal in pMMR LARC, a population previously IO-refractory. Thoracic closed a chapter (concurrent TRT, HR 1.14, null in ES-SCLC chemoIO). Breast offered a practical PK-guided concurrency map for modern systemic agents.

sites.intro: IO breaks into pMMR LARC: node-sparing SCRT + tislelizumab triples pCR rate over conventional chemoradiation.

studies.name: mRCAT-III
studies.tldr: pCR 61% vs 29% (p<0.0001) with node-sparing SCRT + CAPOX + tislelizumab vs conventional SCRT + CAPOX in pMMR LARC.
details: 📊 Severe GI AEs lower in experimental arm per investigator report; specific rates not provided in source
🔍 Phase 3 open-label RCT, N=154 (77/arm), 17 centers China, stratified by cN stage
🔍 Node-sparing RT: 5Gy×5d targeting tumor bed only; elective tumor-draining LNs excluded from CTV
💊 Experimental: tislelizumab 200mg d1 + oxaliplatin 130mg/m² d1 + capecitabine 1000mg/m² d1-14
⚠️ pCR is surrogate; EFS/OS are secondary endpoints, not yet reported
⚠️ Small N (77/arm); single-country (China); open-label (pCR assessed by blinded central review)
⚠️ pMMR/MSS only; dMMR/MSI-H pts excluded (already IO-responsive in LARC)

verdict.rationale: Phase 3 RCT meeting pCR primary endpoint, but EFS/OS not yet reported; small N (77/arm); single-country design.
verdict.audience: pMMR/MSS LARC, cT3-4N0/+M0, tumor ≤10cm from anal verge, no positive lateral LN

open_questions: EFS and OS outcomes (secondary endpoints, not yet reported)
Organ preservation rate with node-sparing approach
Risk of elective nodal failure with lymph node omission

sites.intro: ASCO26 PK framework operationalizes which agents to continue, hold, or sequence around breast and RNI fields.

studies.name: Breast RT + Systemic Therapy Concurrency Review (Speers)
studies.tldr: T-DXd ILD 9.6% vs T-DM1 1.6% (DESTINY-Breast05); ASCO26 PK-guided concurrency framework for breast RT + systemic agents.
details: 🔍 ASCO26 educational review: PK-guided concurrency framework for systemic therapy during breast/CW + RNI (adapted from ASCO Educational Book 2026)

details.text: 💊 Traffic-light summary: concurrent RT safety by agent class
subdetails: CONTINUE: endocrine therapy (minimal radiosensitization), trastuzumab + pertuzumab (standard; no serious AEs concurrent)
CAUTION: T-DXd, CDK4/6i (large fields), T-DM1, pembro, olaparib, mTOR/PI3K agents

details.text: 💊 T-DXd (t½=6d): ILD dominant toxicity; reasonable concurrent with monitoring
subdetails: RT timing in T-DXd arm: 10.7% sequential vs 9.6% concurrent - no effect on ILD
COMBART (40 pts concurrent RT/SRT): acute tox 20%

details: 💊 T-DM1 (t½=4d): CONTINUE during locoregional RT; CNS SRS radionecrosis is the do-not-ignore signal
📊 KEYNOTE-522 post-hoc (1,174 pts, 715 received RT): concurrent pembro + RT numerically fewer G3-5 AEs vs sequential
🔍 P-RAD (TBCRC-053): preop RT (0/9/24 Gy) + pembro in HR+/HER2-; 24 Gy arm: statistically significant T-cell infiltration increase at 2 weeks

subdetails: Olaparib: RT must complete 2-12 wks before starting; RadioPARP suggests concurrent safety in limited settings
Veliparib: AVOID concurrent (TBCRC 024: severe moist desquamation + fibrosis)
Talazoparib (t½=90hr): long PK tail favors sequential strategy
Abemaciclib/ribociclib (t½=24-55hr): hold for large fields; concurrent feasible in trial setting only (NCT05996107)
Palbociclib (t½=28.8hr): practical to hold around RT; resume promptly after

details: ⚠️ All concurrency guidance rests on post-hoc analyses, small prospective series, or retrospective cohorts; PK washout + sequential is safer default outside protocol

figures.caption: DESTINY-Breast05: ILD 9.6% T-DXd vs 1.6% T-DM1. RT timing (T-DXd arm): 10.7% sequential vs 9.6% concurrent. PI (5.4 mg/kg): ILD ~12%, fatal ~0.9%. COMBART 40 pts: acute tox 20%.
KEYNOTE-522 post-hoc: 1,174 pts, 715 received RT. Concurrent pembro + RT: numerically fewer G3-5 AEs vs sequential.

verdict.rationale: Expert narrative review, not a primary trial. Codifies PK-based concurrency guidance consistent with emerging clinical practice.

open_questions: Optimal T-DXd concurrency window as ILD outcomes mature
Prospective CDK4/6i + RT data beyond NCT05996107
Whether P-RAD immune priming translates to survival benefit

sites.intro: CREST-era TRT rationale doesn't survive the IO era; concurrent thoracic RT adds no OS in ES-SCLC chemoIO.

studies.name: ES-SCLC Concurrent TRT + Chemoimmunotherapy (LBA8005)
studies.tldr: mOS 10.0 vs 11.8mo, HR 1.14, p=0.40: concurrent TRT adds no OS benefit to chemoIO in ES-SCLC.
details: 🔍 Phase III RCT, N=228 (115 vs 113); TRT 30 Gy/10 fr concurrent from day 21-28 of cycle 1
🔍 Eligibility: stage IV SCLC or stage III ineligible for curative CRT, ECOG 0-1, measurable thoracic lesion required
🔍 PCI 25-30 Gy optional for responders per local routine; durvalumab 1500 mg Q4W maintenance until PD
🔗 vs CREST (Lancet 2015): TRT 30 Gy/10 fr post-induction improved 1yr OS in pre-IO ES-SCLC; not replicated here in IO era
⚠️ Control arm mOS (11.8mo) consistent with CASPIAN durvalumab arm (~12.9mo); TRT arm numerically inferior at 10.0mo
⚠️ Pre-specified OS subgroups (completed all 4 cycles, no brain/liver mets) both null, consistent with ITT
⚠️ PCI optional per local routine; differential PCI use between arms unreported, potential survival confound

verdict.rationale: Null phase III (HR 1.14 OS, p=0.40); chemoIO alone is IO-era ES-SCLC standard. Confirms omitting TRT is appropriate.
verdict.audience: ES-SCLC, treatment-naïve, ECOG 0-1, measurable thoracic lesion

open_questions: Whether consolidative rather than concurrent TRT sequencing improves outcomes
Role of PCI in IO-era ES-SCLC given uncontrolled differential use in this trial
Optimal ES-SCLC subpopulation (if any) for TRT in the IO era

A Multicenter, Randomized, Open-Label phase 3 Trial
Bingjun Bai, Cheng Cai, Min Chen, Hui Liu, Da Wang, Weifeng Lao, Peng Hu, Xiaonan Sun, Zhentian

Takeaway Points
• This trial provides evidence that combining node-sparing radiotherapy with
tislelizumab and chemotherapy is an effective treatment strategy for patients
with MMR LARC.
• The experimental arm achieved a remarkable doubling of the pCR rate
(61.0% vs 28.6%, P<0.001), meeting the primary endpoint.
• This innovative approach not only improves tumor response but also reduces
the incidence of severe gastrointestinal adverse events, indicating an
improved tolerability profile.
2026 ASCO
ANNUAL MEETING
#ASCO26
Dr. Bai, Bingjun

image_ocr_texts:
HER2-Directed Therapy + Radiation
The mAbs are settled - the ADC question is T-DM1 vs T-DXd
13
V Trastuzumab + Pertuzumab - concurrent
with breast/CW + nodal RT is standard practice
Acceptable toxicity, No serious adverse events with concurrent treatment
HERA trial - trastuzumab initiated after completion of chemotherapy and XRT
Finter trial ART was started after compretion of chemotherapy and rastuzumab
NCCTG N9831 - XRT given concurrently with trastuzumab
APHINITY Trial - trastuzumab and pertuzumab given concurrently with RT
T-DM 1 ado-rastuzumab emtansine; t/½ = 4 days, 5t½ = 20 days
CONTINUE during locoregional RT as on
KATHERINE or ATEMPT Trials
KEY TOXICITY SIGNALS
Dermatitis may be underreported (Corbin)
Pneumonitis small signal - maintain vigilance
CNS radionecrosis with SRS - the "do not ignore" signal
USE CAUTION WITH
large lung volumes • IMN coverage • bolus • reconstruction •
CNS SRS
T-DX trastuzumab deruxtecan; t/½ = 6 days, 5t% = 30 days
REASONABLE with caution - monitor closely
DOMINANT TOXICITY - ILD
PI (5.4 mg/kg): ILD ~12%; fatal ~ 0.9%
DESTINY-Breast05: ILD 9.6% T-DXd vs 1.6% T-DM1
RT timing (T-Dd arm): 10.7% seq vs 9.6% concurrent - no effect
COMBART: 40 pts concurrent RT/SRT; acute tox 20%
SEQUENCE OR HOLD FOR
high lung-dose plans • baseline ILD • thoracic/lung RT •
symptomatic pulmonary disease

KEYNOTE-522 normalized concurrency - P-RAD shows RT is immunomodulatory
14
PK REALITY
Pembrolizumab
t½ = 22 days
5 t½ ~ 110 days
→ Holding by PK rarely practical — risk mitigation shifts
to volume, dose constraints, monitoring
KEYNOTE-522
Pembrolizumab + RT - from sequential to concurrent
V1 Original - restart pembro ≥ 2 wk post-RT
V2 Amended - concurrent pembro + RT permitted
Post-hoc: 1,174 patients • 715 received RT • concurrent pembro + RT well tolerated;
Numerically fewer G3-5 AEs and Immune AEs in concurrent group vs. sequential group;
Highest rate in Pembre alone and no RT arm (maybe RT is immunosuppressive?)
pneumonitis vigilance maintained
McArthur et al. CancerRes2023;83(5_Supplement)PD3-01
P-RAD (TBCRC-053) - RADIATION AS A BIOLOGIC STRESS TEST
Preoperative RT (0 / 9 / 24 Gy) + pembrolizumab in HR+/HER2- disease
24 Gy + pembro
Statistically significant f in T-cell infiltration at 2
weeks
Tertiary lymphoid structures
Induced
- microenvironment converted toward
immune-active state
Implication
Short-course preop RT can prime the immune
system - foundation for RADAR / ART

PARPi, Capecitabine, and CDK4/6i + Radiation
Where caution is non-negotiable - and where to hold
Olaparib
½ = 15 hr, 5t½ = 3 days
OlympiA, RadioPARP, S1706, OlympiAD
RT must complete 2-12 wks BEFORE olaparib
Sequential paradigm in early-stage;
RadioPARP suggests concurrent safety
Tutt et al., NEJM 2021;384:2394-2405
Loap et al., JROBP, 2025 Nov 1: 123(3):726-731
Veliparib
½ = 5.5 hr, 5t½ = 1 day
TBCRC 024
Severe acute + late toxicity with concurrent RT
AVOID concurrency - dose-limiting moist
desquamation, fibrosis
Jagsi et al., J Clin Oncol 2018; 36(13): 1317-1322
Talazoparib
t½ = 90 hr; 5t½ = 19 days
PK tail
Long t½ complicates brief holds
Favors sequential strategy in early-stage
Capecitabine
t½ = ~45 min; 5t½ = 4 hrs
CREATE-X. Phase I/ Concurrent Trial Woodward et al.
Adjuvant paradigm is sequential, not concurrent
AVOID concurrent breast/CW RT outside
protocol; resume after RT
Masuda et al. N Engl J Med. 2017;376(22):2147-2159.
Woodward et al. JROBP. 2017;99(4):777-783.
Abemaciclib / Ribociclib
t½ = 24-55 hr; 5t = 5-11 days
NCT05996107
Limited prospective data; most evidence
retrospective
Hold during RT for large fields;
concurrent feasible in trial setting
Palbociclib
t½ = 28.8 hr 5t½ = 6 days
5 t½ = 6 days
Marrow / GI exposure increases with field size
Practical to hold around RT; resume
promptly after
When in doubt, hold. PK-based washout → RT → resume — the safer default outside protocols

Putting It Together: A Traffic-Light Summary
Concurrent systemic therapy with breast / chest wall + RNI
V CONTINUE
Concurrent with RT
Endocrine therapy
Minimal radiosensitization
Trastuzumab ‡ Pertuzumab
Generally safe; modern heart-sparing
! CAUTION
Plan- / setting-dependent
T-DXd
Concurrent reasonable with monitoring;
sequence/hold for high lung-dose or active
pulmonary disease
CDK4/6 inhibitors
Usually hold for large fields; concurrent only in
selected/protocol settings
X HOLD / SEQUENCE
Outside dedicated protocol
Cytotoxic chemotherapy
Anthracyclines, taxanes, platinum -
sequence, do not give concurrently
Capecitabine / PARPI
(veliparib/talazoparib)
Adjuvant paradigm non-concurrent, veliparib +
RT shows severe acute/late tox
T-DM1
Continue during locoregional RT; dermatitis &
pneumonitis vigilance; caution with CNS SRS
Immunotherapy (Pembro)
Generally tolerable; pneumonitis vigilance +
immune-toxicity workflows
Olaparib
Reasonable with limited fields; concurrent only
in selected/protocol settings
mTOR / PISK agents
Mucosal, skin, metabolic, inflammatory toxicity
risks; ESMO-ESTRO advises caution
